section.heading: Seng Mountain and Bear Creek Scenic Areas, Jefferson National Forest, Virginia

subsection.heading: Establishment
subsection.chapeau: There are designated as National Scenic Areas—

paragraph.content: certain National Forest System land in the Jefferson National Forest, comprising approximately 5,192 acres, as generally depicted on the map entitled “Seng Mountain and Raccoon Branch” and dated April 28, 2008, which shall be known as the “Seng Mountain National Scenic Area”; and

certain National Forest System land in the Jefferson National Forest, comprising approximately 5,128 acres, as generally depicted on the map entitled “Bear Creek” and dated April 28, 2008, which shall be known as the “Bear Creek National Scenic Area”.

subsection.heading: Purposes
subsection.chapeau: The purposes of the scenic areas are—

paragraph.content: to ensure the protection and preservation of scenic quality, water quality, natural characteristics, and water resources of the scenic areas;
consistent with paragraph (1), to protect wildlife and fish habitat in the scenic areas;
to protect areas in the scenic areas that may develop characteristics of old-growth forests; and
consistent with paragraphs (1), (2), and (3), to provide a variety of recreation opportunities in the scenic areas.

paragraph.heading: In general
paragraph.chapeau: The Secretary shall administer the scenic areas in accordance with—

subparagraph.content: the laws (including regulations) generally applicable to the National Forest System.

content.p: The Secretary shall only allow uses of the scenic areas that the Secretary determines will further the purposes of the scenic areas, as described in subsection (b).

Not later than 2 years after March 30, 2009, the Secretary shall develop as an amendment to the land and resource management plan for the Jefferson National Forest a management plan for the scenic areas.
Nothing in this subsection requires the Secretary to revise the land and resource management plan for the Jefferson National Forest under section 1604 of this title.
Except as provided in paragraph (2), after March 30, 2009, no roads shall be established or constructed within the scenic areas.
Nothing in this subsection denies any owner of private land (or an interest in private land) that is located in a scenic area the right to access the private land.
Except as provided in paragraphs (2) and (3), no harvesting of timber shall be allowed within the scenic areas.

paragraph.heading: Exceptions
paragraph.chapeau: The Secretary may authorize harvesting of timber in the scenic areas if the Secretary determines that the harvesting is necessary to—

subparagraph.content: provide for public safety or trail access; or

content.p: Firewood may be harvested for personal use along perimeter roads in the scenic areas, subject to any conditions that the Secretary may impose.

subsection.heading: Insect and disease outbreaks
subsection.chapeau: The Secretary may control insect and disease outbreaks—

paragraph.content: to reduce hazards to visitors; or

content.p: The Secretary may engage in vegetation manipulation practices in the scenic areas to maintain the visual quality and wildlife clearings in existence on March 30, 2009.
Except as provided in paragraph (2), motorized vehicles shall not be allowed within the scenic areas.

paragraph.heading: Exceptions
paragraph.chapeau: The Secretary may authorize the use of motorized vehicles—

subparagraph.content: to carry out administrative activities that further the purposes of the scenic areas, as described in subsection (b);
to assist wildlife management projects in existence on March 30, 2009; and

subparagraph.chapeau: during deer and bear hunting seasons—

clause.content: on Forest Development Roads 49410 and 84b; and
on the portion of Forest Development Road 6261 designated on the map described in subsection (a)(2) as “open seasonally”.

subsection.heading: Wildfire suppression
subsection.chapeau: Wildfire suppression within the scenic areas shall be conducted—

paragraph.content: in a manner consistent with the purposes of the scenic areas, as described in subsection (b); and
using such means as the Secretary determines to be appropriate.

content.p: The Secretary shall administer the scenic areas in a manner that maintains and enhances water quality.

subsection.heading: Withdrawal
subsection.chapeau: Subject to valid existing rights, all Federal land in the scenic areas is withdrawn from—

paragraph.content: location, entry, and patent under the mining laws; and
operation of the mineral leasing and geothermal leasing laws.

note.heading: References in Text
note.p: This subtitle, referred to in subsec. (c)(1)(A), is subtitle B (§§ 1101–1107) of title I of Pub. L. 111–11, Mar. 30, 2009, 123 Stat. 1002, which enacted this section and section 546b–1 of this title, enacted provisions set out as a note below, and enacted and amended provisions listed in a table of Wilderness Areas set out under section 1132 of this title. For complete classification of subtitle B to the Code, see Tables.

note.heading: Definitions
note.p:
Pub. L. 111–11, title I, § 1101, Mar. 30, 2009, 123 Stat. 1002, provided that: “In this subtitle [subtitle B (§§ 1101–1107) of title I of Pub. L. 111–11, enacting this section and section 546b–1 of this title and enacting and amending provisions listed in a table of Wilderness Areas set out under section 1132 of this title]: “(1) Scenic areas.—The term ‘scenic areas’ means the Seng Mountain National Scenic Area and the Bear Creek National Scenic Area.
“(2) Secretary.—The term ‘Secretary’ means the Secretary of Agriculture.”
